text: Ši na, wo̱mĩfĩmo̱ ṅmā̃kei ye̱ ṅmõši, ni na, mike le̱ te ši damo̱ ši, ni na, nyekei le̱ babo̱le mike le̱ ni ame̱kotole̱.
translated: And now, our brothers and sisters, we want you to know about those who have fallen asleep, so that you do not grieve like the rest who have no hope.

text: Ni enyemimei le̱ kē̱le̱ ake̱: Aso maṅtše̱ po okē̱ ooye ye̱ wo̱no̱ ne̱? alo ye obaye wo̱no̱ ne̱? Ni ame̱nye̱le̱ fè tšutšu le̱ ye̱ elamõi le̱ ke̱ ewiemo̱i le̱ ahewo̱.

text: Ni eba mli ake̱, beni Iosef še̱ enyemimei le̱ aṅõ le̱, ame̱kplo̱ etade ni dši atade wuyã fe̱fe̱o ni ewo le̱ ye̱ ehe, K.

text: Ke̱ke̱ ni Iuda kē̱ ešāyō Tamar ake̱: Yahĩ ši ake̱ okũlafo ye̱ otše̱ we le̱ ke̱-yaši beyino̱ ni mibi Šela lē̱ aada. Edšake̱ ekē̱: Koni le̱ hũ ekabagbo tamo̱ enyemimei le̱. Ni Tamar te eyahĩ etše̱ we le̱.
translated: Then Judah said to his daughter-in-law Tamar: "Live as a widow in your father's house until my son Shelah grows up." For he thought: "He might die too, like his brothers." So Tamar went and lived in her father's house.

ref: Hirah asked the people of Enaim, “Where is the prostitute who sat along the road outside your town?” “There's never been one here,” they answered.
text: Ke̱ke̱ ni eli dše̱ibii ke̱ ake̱: Nẽgbe̱ yō adſamaṅ ní tá Enaim gbe̱ le̱ toi le̱ yo̱? Ni ame̱ke: Yō adſamaṅ ko be̱ bie̱ ne̱.
translated: And he asked the men of that place, saying: Where is the harlot that was in Enaim by the wayside? And they said: There was no harlot in this place.

ref: At once his hand went back in, and the other child was born first. The woman then said, “What an opening you've made for yourself!” So they named the baby Perez.

translated: And when her days to be delivered were fulfilled, behold, there were twins in her womb. And the first came out red, all over like an hairy garment; and they called his name Esau. And after that came his brother out, and his hand took hold on Esau's heel; and his name was called Jacob: and Isaac was threescore years old when she bare them. And the boys grew: and Esau was a cunning hunter, a man of the field; and Jacob was a plain man, dwelling in tents. And Isaac loved Esau, because he did eat of his venison: but Rebekah loved Jacob. And Jacob sod pottage: and Esau came from the field, and he was faint: And Esau said to Jacob, Feed me, I pray thee, with that same red pottage; for I am faint: therefore was his name called Edom. And Jacob said, Sell me this day thy birthright. And Esau said, Behold, I am at the point to die: and what profit shall this birthright do to me? And Jacob said, Swear to me this day; and he sware unto him: and he sold his birthright unto Jacob. Then Jacob gave Esau bread and pottage of lentiles; and he did eat and drink, and rose up, and went his way: thus Esau despised his birthright. Mat.

translated: When Joseph was unable to hold back his feelings any longer, he sent everyone out; only he remained.

text: Ni etšṍ ehe ke̱-dše ame̱ṅõ ni eyafó; ni ekũ ese̱ eba ame̱ṅõ ekoṅ ni ekeame̱ bawie; ni edšie Simeon ye̱ ame̱teṅ, ni efĩle ye̱ ame̱hĩe.
translated: And they took him and cast him into a pit; and the pit was empty, there was no water in it; and they sat down to eat bread, and they lifted up their eyes and looked, and behold a company of Ishmaelites came from Gilead with their camels bearing spicery and balm and myrrh, going to carry it down to Egypt. And Judah said unto his brethren, What profit is it if we slay our brother, and conceal his blood? text: Ni awadše Farao tšui, ni ebooame̱ toi, take̱ bo̱ni Iehowa *ewie le̱. *
